description:
Add a #[serde(flatten)] extra: BTreeMap<String, Value> catch-all to the Task struct (and any sibling types serialized to the state branch) so mixed-version bl clients reading state-branch commits round-trip unknown fields rather than silently dropping them.

## Why now (independent of bl-b7fb)

bl-2148 just shipped: state-branch commits are now actively pushed to remotes and consumed by other machines. Until this lands, any future field added by a newer bl is at risk of being silently dropped on the next save by an older client. That's data loss, and it is no longer hypothetical.

This is filed under bl-b7fb because every subsequent child of that epic adds new fields somewhere in the schema (config-side mostly, but the safety net should be in place first). It is also independently useful and should not block on the SPEC.

## Scope

- Task struct (src/task.rs) gains an `extra` catch-all.
- Audit other state-branch-serialized types (links, deps, claim records) for the same exposure; add catch-alls where the type can grow.
- Tests: round-trip a Task with synthetic future-version unknown fields; write-then-read across a simulated version mismatch (older serializer must preserve fields written by newer); ensure existing equality / display logic is unaffected by the presence of extra entries.

## Out of scope

- New fields themselves. This is purely the safety net.
title: Forward-compat Task deserializer with extra-field catch-all
